title: alyssum alpestre var. serpyllifolium
intro: Alyssum alpestre var. serpyllifolium, also known as mountain alyssum, is a type of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its small white blooms and narrow, grey-green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic. It is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Alyssum alpestre var. serpyllifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in topical applications and salves for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
